review_questions: Does CP00-148 cover exactly the 10 planned Risk A failure boundary units in order?
Can any CP00-148 row emit blocked-claim receipts, Hermes failure evidence, audit failure hints, or write audit/product state?
Can any CP00-148 row load fixtures, write failure fixtures, execute unit/integration tests, or execute failure recovery?
Can any CP00-148 row send Claude prompts, record human escalation notes, render UI, call APIs, or expose hidden failure internals?
Does CP00-148 hand off cleanly from CP00-147 to CP00-149 without implementing LDIP or splitting HRX?

parsed_review.summary: Reviewed CP00-148 Risk A failure-boundary-sensitive closeout pack covering RP03.P07.M03.S15-RP03.P07.M04.S02 (10 units: 8 primary sensitive-boundary M03 slices S15-S22 + 2 secondary workflow M04 slices S01-S02). The new packages/audit/src/failure-boundary-sensitive-catalog.js is strictly descriptor/reference-only: CP148_UNITS/CP148_ROWS are static frozen metadata, and freezeNoWriteResult spreads the result then unconditionally overrides every side-effect flag to false (writes_product_state, appends/writes/mutates/deletes_audit_event, evaluates_failure_taxonomy, executes_failure_recovery, throws_failure, executes_retry_exhaustion/rollback_expectation/compensation, emits_blocked_claim_receipt/hermes_failure_evidence/audit_failure_hint, writes_failure_fixture, executes_failure_unit_test/integration_smoke, materializes_claude_edge_case_prompt, sends_claude_prompt, records/executes_human_escalation, executes_api_handler, issues_network_request, renders_ui/mutates_dom/executes_ui_interaction, loads/reads/materializes fixture payloads, implements_ldip, splits_hrx_product) so no row can write audit/product state, execute recovery, emit receipts/evidence, run tests, send Claude prompts, record escalation, call APIs, or render UI. Hermes packet returns empty blocked_claims and only records command labels (no execution); Claude packet is read_only with executes_review and sends_claude_prompt false. Plan binding verified: validateAuditComplianceCp148Coverage compares covered IDs against plan included_units in order, and pack_id/risk_class A/unit_count 10/range/first+last unit all match. Handoff chain correct: upstream CP00-147 -> CP00-148 -> next CP00-149 at RP03.P07.M04.S03. Contract bumped v0.14->v0.15 with current_pack, failure_boundary_sensitive_pack, 11 public_exports, 25 hidden_source_fields, and the no_write_attestation all consistent with the module and enforced by the validator. H03/C03 gates bound (evidence id H03.CP00-148..., review id C03.CP00-148...); hidden-field redaction policy declares 25 field names (names only, no values) and case results assert hidden_field_names_exposed/unauthorized_count/object_name_exposed false. Deliverable distribution (hermes_evidence 2, fixture 1, test 2, security_audit 1, claude_review 1, implementation 1, failure_recovery 2) and evidence-mode split (8 primary / 2 secondary) match tests. Four new tests plus extensive validator additions cover coverage, distributions, per-case no-write, manifest, Hermes/Claude/handoff. All stated validations (node --check, JSON.parse, node --test CP00-148, rp03:audit:validate, closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate) passed. No P0-P3 findings; the descriptor-only boundary is fully intact and consistent with the CP00-135..CP00-147 pattern.
